name: Raising
languages: Middle English to Early Modern English (Great Vowel Shift)

name: The shift from /ai/ to /au/ constitutes a backing and rounding of the second vocalic element of the diphthong
This shift primarily involves the fronting and unrounding of the diphthong's off-glide (the second element)
This transformation involves several key phonetic effects: fronting
The transformation from /au/ to /oi/ involves a chain of vowel shifts
This shift encompasses devoicing (from voiced to voiceless)
Velarization (change of place of articulation from bilabial to velar) and Devoicing
Palatalization
languages: Old Mandarin to Modern Mandarin Chinese

name: Backing
languages: Inland Northern American English (Buffalo, Cleveland, Detroit, Chicago)

name: Backing
languages: Old High German to Modern German
